routing.task_class: Explicit Agentic Runner workflow-state and source CLI operation
routing.primary_route: Use the source CLI and the agentic-runner skill only after an explicit Agentic Runner request or an explicit request to continue, audit, or repair its state.
fallback_conditions: Continue in the main thread when the official subagent workflow is unavailable or the work is tightly coupled.
Use a specialist leaf workflow directly when the request is single-domain and does not explicitly select Agentic Runner.
non_use_conditions: Do not auto-route generic orchestration, coding, debugging, delegation, handoff, or mixed work to Agentic Runner.
Do not treat plugin cache state as the editable source of truth.

inputs.name: Explicit workflow request
inputs.description: A named Agentic Runner request or an explicit continuation, audit, or repair target with task, epoch, and scope.

inputs.name: Target Git worktree
inputs.description: The resolved jobsite repository whose local .agentic-runner state is inspected or updated.

outputs.name: Workflow state
outputs.description: Scoped .agentic-runner planning, assignment, lifecycle, handoff, and audit records.

outputs.name: CLI validation evidence
outputs.description: Doctor, assignment verification, tests, and source versus cache boundary evidence.

rules.description: Agentic Runner remains explicit-only and preserves declared specialist ownership instead of auto-routing generic work.
Workflow writes stay inside the target Git worktree's ignored .agentic-runner state and runner scope checks reject out-of-scope changes.
Current workflow packets enforce lifecycle, supervision, metacognitive, and contract-coverage completion evidence.
